title: Docker client v29 now requires Docker server v25
body:
### Feature request description

The newest docker client can no longer use podman server.

`API version 1.41 is not supported by this client: the minimum supported API version is 1.44`

https://github.com/moby/moby/commit/2c59be7011867517a76fbce9a47387c3e95667ae

### Suggest potential solution

The "CurrentAPI" version needs to be bumped, from 1.41 to 1.44

This means that API changes in 1.42, 1.43, 1.44 needs addressing

*  https://docs.docker.com/reference/api/engine/version-history/#v142-api-changes
*  https://docs.docker.com/reference/api/engine/version-history/#v143-api-changes
*  https://docs.docker.com/reference/api/engine/version-history/#v144-api-changes

### Have you considered any alternatives?

A shortterm workaround is downgrading the docker client to v25.

Using the libpod socket instead of the compat socket is another.

### Additional context

* https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/27877

comments.body: @afbjorklund would you have time to propose a PR?

The strange thing is that I thought I saw some 1.44 code already?

1.
commit b7b7839ad3bfabab74aa618aec491f5d3e5a6578

Why is there code for VirtualSize, if the newest version was 1.41?

EDIT: There is even code for 1.45, `rg apiutil.SupportedVersion`

2.
commit 571866775d830a9e0e2de719463c665f909c9bb1

Similar for the Container and ContainerConfig, moved to Config

EDIT: And also code for 1.42, with the http header for `attach`

3.
commit 7c9c969815d7c99212198b4b87fad435cdd89971

Is the version negotiation code buggy in podman, somehow?

---

But the oneliner to bump the API is the simple part, verifying...

```diff
--- a/version/version.go
+++ b/version/version.go
@@ -40,7 +40,7 @@ var APIVersion = map[Tree]map[Level]semver.Version{
                MinimalAPI: semver.MustParse("4.0.0"),
        },
        Compat: {
-               CurrentAPI: semver.MustParse("1.41.0"),
+               CurrentAPI: semver.MustParse("1.44.0"),
                MinimalAPI: semver.MustParse("1.24.0"),
        },
 }
```

I updated the version to 1.44, hoping that the already added tests would cover the removed fields...

It seems like Moby wants to remove support for **all** older versions, so might need to bump to 1.47

https://docs.docker.com/reference/api/engine/version-history/#v147-api-changes
https://docs.docker.com/reference/api/engine/version-history/#v146-api-changes
https://docs.docker.com/reference/api/engine/version-history/#v145-api-changes

"The intent is to start deprecating older API versions when daemons implementing them reach EOL. .."

https://github.com/moby/moby/blob/master/project/BRANCHES-AND-TAGS.md

@baude This means that API version will needs to be updated much more often, to follow Moby releases?

"... should be considered "best effort", and we may remove compatibility code to provide "degraded" support."

----

Currently the v25/1.44 API is tolerated, because it is supported by the Mirantis MCR product (nee Docker Enterprise)

Upgrading from 1.41 to 1.44 fixes the issue with Docker 29.0, so it's enough for now...

But _eventually_ might want to upgrade to Docker 27.5, the EOL version that is used in for instance Debian "testing"

And there probably need to be a _lot_ more tests, for every Docker Engine API version.

It was stable for a while (after the initial versions, like 1.40-1.45), but then the changes started accelerating again...

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c94e8fc2-b4aa-4ab6-a7a9-17837f170156)

See https://docs.docker.com/reference/api/engine/#api-version-matrix and https://endoflife.date/docker-engine
